evals.prompt: Analyze PPA options for a 30MW data center facility in ERCOT (Texas). Current grid tariff is $45/MWh. A solar PPA has been offered at $35/MWh with 2% annual escalator over 15 years, physical delivery. Self-generation solar option available with on-site land. ERCOT curtailment rates are 3-5% for solar. The operator has RE100 commitments requiring additionality.
evals.expected_output: A PPA analysis showing: PPA cheaper than grid on levelized basis, basis risk quantified for ERCOT market (typically $2-5/MWh), curtailment rate modeled at 3-5% reflecting Texas solar/wind market, levelized cost comparison table with grid vs PPA all-in (including basis risk and curtailment) vs self-gen LCOE, additionality assessment for RE100 compliance, and sensitivity to curtailment and grid escalation.
assertions: PPA is cheaper than grid on levelized cost basis
Basis risk is quantified for ERCOT (node-to-hub spread)
Curtailment rate reflects Texas market (3-5%)
Levelized cost comparison table includes all three options
Additionality assessment addresses RE100 requirements
Sensitivity analysis varies curtailment, grid escalation, and basis risk
Both Financial Disclaimer and Tax Disclaimer are included
Self-gen LCOE includes ITC benefit

evals.prompt: Analyze PPA options for a 10MW data center in PJM (Virginia). Current grid tariff is $65/MWh. A virtual PPA (financial settlement) has been offered at $50/MWh with 1.5% escalator over 12 years. PJM is a congested market with high basis risk. No on-site self-generation option (urban site). The operator needs Scope 2 market-based accounting for ESG reporting.
evals.expected_output: A PPA analysis showing: basis risk is significant in congested PJM ($5-8/MWh), virtual PPA accounting treatment note (ASC 815 derivative), net PPA cost higher than headline $50/MWh price after basis risk, additionality assessment for Scope 2 market-based reporting, grid remains competitive when PPA all-in cost is calculated. Self-gen shows as not viable due to urban site constraints.
assertions: Basis risk is significant for PJM ($5-8/MWh range)
Virtual PPA accounting treatment (ASC 815) is noted
Net PPA cost exceeds headline price after basis risk adder
Additionality assessment addresses Scope 2 market-based accounting
Self-gen viability is limited by urban site constraints
Levelized comparison shows PPA vs grid with basis risk included
Sensitivity analysis shows basis risk as critical variable
Financial and Tax disclaimers are included

evals.prompt: Analyze energy procurement for a 50MW hyperscale data center in Northern Europe. Grid tariff is $80/MWh (carbon-inclusive). A wind PPA is offered at $55/MWh with 1% escalator over 20 years. Self-generation wind option available with high 35% capacity factor. ITC is not applicable (non-US jurisdiction). CSRD additionality requirements apply. Nordic curtailment rates are low (1-2%).
evals.expected_output: A PPA analysis showing: international tariff handling (no US-specific tax credits), self-gen competitive at hyperscale with high capacity factor, ITC correctly excluded for non-US, CSRD additionality requirements noted for EU compliance, wind PPA competitive against high grid tariff, low curtailment reduces PPA risk, sensitivity to curtailment and grid carbon pricing changes.
assertions: International tariff handling without US-specific assumptions
Self-gen is competitive at 50MW scale with 35% wind capacity factor
ITC is correctly excluded for non-US jurisdiction
CSRD additionality requirements are specifically addressed
Wind PPA is competitive against carbon-inclusive grid tariff
Low curtailment (1-2%) improves PPA economics
Sensitivity analysis includes curtailment variation
Financial Disclaimer is included
